weather.summary: Saturday: 12°C, scattered clouds. Sunday: 13°C, broken clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend looks perfect for exploring! I've found some fantastic free activities, including museum visits and playground adventures. Plus, there are some great Easter events to book ahead for some springtime fun.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: A relaxed space for little ones to discover new books and enjoy stories.

transport.fromHackney: Bus routes and Overground to Hackney Downs or Hackney Central.
transport.tip: A great option for a rainy afternoon.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Interactive galleries designed for children to imagine, play and design.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on exhibits and a Mini Museum area perfect for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Bethnal Green, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check out the Sound Explorers sessions for babies and toddlers.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A small, beautifully designed wooden playground with a pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect for toddlers with its gentle features and natural setting.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes to Stratford International. Then a short walk.
transport.tip: Combine with a walk around the Olympic Park.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Immersive play spaces and a story garden for creative exploration.
activities.whyItWorks: Story Worlds and a playground offer hours of fun and imaginative play.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line or Overground to Stratford.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Discover London's history through interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed with children in mind, featuring hands-on activities and child-friendly explanations. There is also a soft play area Mudlarks.

transport.fromHackney: Jubilee Line to Canary Wharf.
transport.tip: Not usually too crowded.

activities.title: Walk Through Victoria Park
activities.description: Large park with playgrounds and splash pool.
activities.whyItWorks: Plenty of space to run around and play, and a splash pool for warmer days.

transport.fromHackney: Bus routes 277, 425, 388, D6.
transport.tip: Pack a picnic.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: Meet farm animals in the heart of the city.
activities.whyItWorks: A chance to see animals and learn about farming.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes to Haggerston.
transport.tip: Great for a quick visit.

activities.title: The Waterworks
activities.description: Jets and sprays that children can enjoy.
activities.whyItWorks: Great way for kids to cool off.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes to Stratford International. Then a short walk.
transport.tip: Bring a change of clothes.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, mammals, and more in this iconic museum.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaur exhibits and interactive displays make it a hit with kids.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Avoid weekends and school holidays for fewer crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works with interactive exhibits and space exploration galleries.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden and Pattern Pod are great for younger children, while older kids will love the space exhibits.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book tickets to Wonderlab for hands-on experiments.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small zoo with rare species of birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Free entry and a manageable size make it perfect for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Gospel Oak, then Overground to Hampstead Heath, then bus 210.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: One of London's most iconic museums where you can explore world art and culture.
activities.whyItWorks: The Egyptian galleries with mummies and hieroglyphs are captivating for children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington. Then take the Victoria Line to Euston. Then take the Northern Line to Tottenham Court Road.
transport.tip: Visit the family desk for activity sheets and Explorer Backpacks.

activities.title: National Maritime Museum
activities.description: Explore maritime history with child-friendly activities and exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: The All Hands Children's Gallery and Character Encounters on Saturdays are great for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park, then Jubilee Line to North Greenwich, then bus 188.
transport.tip: Check out the role-play space for under 8s.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: A family-friendly museum with gardens, animals, and a musical instrument collection.
activities.whyItWorks: The gardens, farm animals, and interactive exhibits make it a great day out for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: Enjoy the views over London from the gardens.

activities.title: Changing of the Guard, Buckingham Palace
activities.description: Witness the traditional Changing of the Guard ceremony.
activities.whyItWorks: A classic London experience that is visually engaging for children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Victoria.
transport.tip: Check the schedule in advance as it doesn't happen every day.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A pirate-themed playground inspired by Peter Pan.
activities.whyItWorks: The playground is designed to be accessible and inclusive for all children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central line to Notting Hill Gate, then walk through Kensington Gardens.
transport.tip: The Peter Pan statue is worth a visit.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace - Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues in the gardens and win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun Easter-themed activity in a historic setting.

transport.fromHackney: Train from Liverpool Street to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, as this event is very popular.

activities.title: London Zoo - Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Discover animal-themed challenges and hidden eggs around the zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun way to explore the zoo and learn about animals.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Euston, then Northern Line to Camden Town.
transport.tip: Book tickets online for a discount.

activities.title: Hobbledown Heath - Easter Eggsplorers
activities.description: Themed egg trail, indoor/outdoor play, animal meets.
activities.whyItWorks: Plenty of Easter fun with entertainment and interactive moments.

transport.fromHackney: Train from Liverpool Street to Feltham, then bus H26.
transport.tip: Families spend 4-5 hours on average.

activities.title: Tower Bridge - Moving Pictures
activities.description: Craft Victorian phenakistoscope animations of Tower Bridge.
activities.whyItWorks: STEAM magic through Engine Rooms, Glass Floors, and cat-trail stamps.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then bus 35 or 47.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.description: Follow the trail to spot hidden eggs around the ship and unscramble the secret nautical code.
activities.whyItWorks: Discover fascinating facts about Cutty Sark's history and epic voyages.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Cutty Sark.
transport.tip: Claim a chocolatey treat after cracking the code.

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: Join Alice and tumble down the rabbit hole into the fantastical world of Wonderland.
activities.whyItWorks: An exciting show for all the family.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park, then Piccadilly Line to Hammersmith.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Fairytales
activities.description: This immersive family exhibition invites children to step into the magical world of classic fairy tales through beautiful illustrations, pop-up books, original manuscripts and even puppets.
activities.whyItWorks: There are interactive elements throughout – including a magic mirror, a genie and even magical potions – making it more than just a display to look at.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Euston.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Peppa Pig's egg-stra special Easter Afternoon Tea Bus Tour
activities.description: A Peppa Pig themed afternoon tea will be run with special Easter travel cup and Easter Egg hunt activity on a London bus.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun for Peppa Pig fans.

transport.fromHackney: Check departure point when booking.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.
